title: Trust boundary first
description: Identify assets, actors, trust boundaries, and attacker-controlled inputs before proposing a security-sensitive change.
body: Use when a task touches authentication, authorization, parsing, rendering, command execution, network calls, dependency loading, or secret handling. Do not use as a substitute for framework-specific implementation rules or a full threat model for a new product. Actions: map actor, asset, entry point, transformation, sink, and enforcement point before editing; refuse to treat trusted UI state or client claims as authority. Validation signals include negative tests at the boundary, log or trace evidence showing server-side enforcement, and review notes identifying the denied path. Risk boundary: if the change expands access, crosses tenants, or introduces a new externally reachable sink, require human approval before accepting the design.
